message:
fix(engine): implement frame-isolated register addressing in baseline interpreter

The interpreter was using a flat register space for all call frames,
meaning nested function calls would silently overwrite the caller's
registers when writing argument values. This caused incorrect return
values and data corruption in programs with call depth > 1.

Changes:
- Add register_base field to InterpreterCore to track the current frame's
  base offset into the register array
- On function call: save current register_base in the CallFrame, then
  advance register_base by max_registers to give the callee its own
  isolated register window
- On return: restore register_base from the popped CallFrame
- read_reg/write_reg now apply register_base offset, with bounds checking
  against max_registers before computing the actual index
- Snapshot argument values before pushing the call frame to avoid reading
  from the callee's register space after base offset changes
- write_reg grows the register vec on demand when deeper call frames
  address higher indices
- Remove dead_code allow on CallFrame::register_base since it is now
  actively used for frame isolation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
